The **2019 NRL season** was the 112th season of professional [rugby league](/wiki/Rugby_league "Rugby league") in Australia and the 22nd season run by the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League"). The [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") became the first team to win back-to-back Premierships since the [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") in 1997–98, and the first to achieve the feat since the unified NRL competition started in 1998.

## Pre-season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2019_NRL_season&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Pre-season")]

The 2019 pre-season featured the [All Stars match](/wiki/2019_All_Stars_match "2019 All Stars match") held on February 15 at [AAMI Park](/wiki/Melbourne_Rectangular_Stadium "Melbourne Rectangular Stadium").[[16]](#cite_note-16) The [2019 World Club Challenge](/wiki/2019_World_Club_Challenge "2019 World Club Challenge") match saw the [NRL premiers](/wiki/2018_NRL_season "2018 NRL season") [Sydney Roosters](/wiki/Sydney_Roosters "Sydney Roosters") defeat the [Super League champions](/wiki/Super_League_XXIII "Super League XXIII") [Wigan Warriors](/wiki/Wigan_Warriors "Wigan Warriors").

## Regular season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2019_NRL_season&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Regular season")]

For the first time, the NRL announced that the entirety of a round of games would be hosted at a single venue, adopting the Super League's [Magic Weekend](/wiki/Magic_Weekend "Magic Weekend") concept. [Magic Round](/wiki/Magic_Round_(NRL) "Magic Round (NRL)") took place during Round 9 at [Suncorp Stadium](/wiki/Lang_Park "Lang Park").[[17]](#cite_note-17)

State of Origin was played in Perth for the first time, with Game 2 of the [2019 series](/wiki/2019_State_of_Origin_series "2019 State of Origin series") played at [Optus Stadium](/wiki/Perth_Stadium "Perth Stadium").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

See also: [2019 NRL season results](/wiki/2019_NRL_season_results "2019 NRL season results")

## Finals series

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2019_NRL_season&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Finals series")]

[Parramatta Eels](/wiki/Parramatta_Eels "Parramatta Eels") 58 – 0 Elimination Final win over [Brisbane Broncos](/wiki/Brisbane_Broncos "Brisbane Broncos") broke the record for the biggest winning margin in Finals history, beating the previous mark of 48 points when [Newtown](/wiki/Newtown_Jets "Newtown Jets") defeated [St. George](/wiki/St._George_Dragons "St. George Dragons") 55 – 7 in the [1944](/wiki/1944_NSWRFL_season "1944 NSWRFL season") Semi-finals.
